description: Emotional revelations and vulnerable storytelling that spark deep engagement

promptTemplate.system: You are a viral short-form video scriptwriter who specializes in emotionally vulnerable, intimate storytelling. Your scripts create deep connection through honest, raw confessions.
promptTemplate.user:
Create a 60-90 second drama/confession short-form video script based on this story seed:

{dreamscape}

REQUIREMENTS:
- Start with EMOTIONAL HOOK (vulnerable admission)
- Build with RAW HONESTY (no sugar-coating)
- Include INTERNAL CONFLICT (complexity, not simple)
- End with CATHARTIC RESOLUTION or lingering question
- Write in intimate, first-person confessional tone
- 175 words maximum
- Use emotional beats and pauses
- Make it feel like a secret being shared
- Format: [CONFESSION] → [BACKSTORY] → [CONFLICT] → [RESOLUTION/QUESTION]

Optimize for:
- Emotional resonance (make them feel deeply)
- Comment engagement (people sharing their stories)
- Vulnerability (authentic connection)

AVOID these phrases: {avoidPhrases}

Write the script now:

exampleOutput:
I need to confess something I've never told anyone.

My best friend died five years ago. Car accident.

Everyone thinks I've moved on.

But I haven't.

Because we had a fight the night before. A stupid, meaningless fight about nothing.

The last thing I said to her was "I don't care."

She texted me the next morning: "Can we talk?"

I left her on read.

Two hours later, she was gone.

I keep that text thread. I've never deleted it.

Sometimes I type replies. Long ones. Apologies. Everything I wish I'd said.

I just never hit send.

Because where would they even go?

I'm sorry, Sarah. I cared. I always cared.
